# Resource (rootly_edge_connector)



## Example Usage

```shell
resource "rootly_edge_connector" "us_east_datacenter" {
  name = "US-East Datacenter"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Connector name

### Optional

- `description` (String) Connector description
- `status` (String) Connector status. Value must be one of `active`, `paused`.
- `subscriptions` (List of String) Array of event types to subscribe to

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `online` (Boolean) Whether connector is currently online
- `updated_at` (String)

## Import

rootly_edge_connector can be imported using the [`import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import).

```sh
terraform import rootly_edge_connector.primary a816421c-6ceb-481a-87c4-585e47451f24
```

Or using an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import).

```terraform
import {
  to = rootly_edge_connector.primary
  id = "a816421c-6ceb-481a-87c4-585e47451f24"
}
```

Locate the resource id in the web app, or retrieve it by listing resources through the API if it's not visible in the web app.

HCL can be generated from the import block using the `-generate-config-out` flag.

```sh
terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf
```
